mechanism: A mediating chain in which agent characteristics (personalness of source) influence intermediate cognitive process (elaboration of received message) which determines behavioral output (participation likelihood), with moderation by a third characteristic (perceived credibility) that conditions the magnitude of indirect effects at different levels

Extraneous affective state (induced bodily sensation) influences evaluative judgment (moral severity rating) through use of that state as informational input, with individual differences in sensitivity to bodily cues moderating strength of the effect

Automatic behavioral tendency (toward self-serving dishonesty) is overcome by deliberative cognitive control when sufficient time is available and no private justifications exist to rationalize the unethical behavior; time pressure amplifies automatic tendency by preventing deliberation

Inhibitory control mechanism (suppression-induced forgetting) fails to suppress episodic memory retrieval when confronted with aversive reminders in individuals with compromised prefrontal regulation; degree of suppression deficit correlates with symptom severity
domain: memory suppression trauma PTSD inhibitory control

mechanism: Object-based gestalt information (connectedness, closure, continuity between parts) directly drives holistic perceptual grouping via bottom-up mechanism independent of expertise; this same computation operates whether applied to faces or geometric patterns with equivalent information structure

Parental goal-frame regarding setbacks (debilitating vs. enhancing) manifests in visible behavioral responses to child failure which children perceive and internalize, shaping their own goal-frames; relationship is fully mediated by child's perception of parent's performance versus learning orientation
